Description:
This lesson introduces learners to the fundamental components that make up a computer system. It explains the purpose and function of each part– both hardware and software–and how they work together to process information.


Learning Objectives:

This lesson aims to:

1. identify and describe the hardware and software of a computer;

2. apply basic troubleshooting for common problems encountered; and

3. demonstrate proper maintenance and best practices.

sections.title: Topic 1: The Hardware Components of a Computer

sections.content:
Learning the basics of computers may seem complicated on the inside, but every device—from the smallest smartphone to the largest desktop—relies on the same essential building blocks. These building blocks are called hardware components, the physical parts of a computer that you can see and touch.[1][2]

Computer hardware is everything from the central processing unit (CPU) to random access memory (RAM), storage devices (like hard disk drives and solid-state drives), motherboards, power supplies, and input/output devices (such as keyboards, mice, and printers). Each component has a specific function and plays an integral role in the overall performance of the computer.[1][2]

Source: https://scot-comp.co.uk/

A computer is an electronic device that accepts data, performs operations, displays results, and stores the data or results as needed. It is a combination of hardware and software resources that integrate and provide various functionalities to the user. Hardware is the physical components of a computer, such as a processor, memory devices, monitor, keyboard, etc., while software is a set of programs or instructions that are required by the hardware resources to function properly.[1]

In this lesson, you will learn about the different parts and components that make up a desktop computer unit. It is important for us to know the basic parts of the computer hardware, so that you can establish your understanding and keep up for the following lessons in ModuLearn. Along with it, you will learn their functions and parts as well. To further prepare your learning drive, here is an overview of what we will be discussing along the lesson:

Central processing unit (CPU) - known as the ‘brain’ of the computer because it executes instructions and carries out commands.[image-33]

Motherboard - provides the structure for all other components and connects them, while also providing a way to distribute power, deliver information and connect to devices such as a printer or mouse.[image-34]

Random Access Memory (RAM) - is where data lives temporarily while it’s being actively used by programs, such as when a user launches a computer application.[image-35]

sections.content:
Do you know that a computer cannot function without a CPU, even if it has a monitor, keyboard, and a mouse? Just like the human brain, the CPU controls all the activities of the computer. Without it, the system cannot think, calculate, or respond.

In computer hardware servicing, knowing how the CPU works is essential for installation, troubleshooting, upgrading, and maintenance.        This is the main processing component of a computer. It executes instructions from programs and controls all hardware operations.[3]

Arithmetic/Logic Unit

This part of the CPU is responsible for doing calculations and making decisions based on comparisons. Its math functionality is based on four types of operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication and division). Logical operations typically involve some type of comparison (checking whether something is greater than, less than, or equal to something else) that is tied to a particular computer action.[3][4] In simple terms, the ALU is the “calculator and decision maker” of the CPU.

Control Unit (CU)

The Control Unit acts like a manager or traffic controller inside the CPU. The control unit of the CPU houses circuitry (electronic pathways that allow electricity to move and control components) that guides the computer system through a system of electrical pulses (tiny bursts of electrical signals used to send instructions) and notifies it to execute high-level computer instructions (commands given by programs or software).[3][4] Simply put, the CU tells all the other parts of the computer what to do and when to do it.

Memory Unit

The Memory Unit is responsible for handling data and instructions while the CPU is working. It handles several key functions related to memory usage, from managing the data flow (movement of information) that occurs between RAM and the CPU to overseeing the important work of the cache memory (a small, very fast memory used to store frequently used data). Memory units contain all types of data and instructions needed for data processing (handling and organizing information) and offer memory-protection safeguards (features that prevent programs from interfering with each other’s data).[3][4] In simple terms, the Memory Unit keeps important data ready so the CPU can use it quickly and safely.

sections.content:
If the CPU is the brain, the motherboard is the body’s nervous system. Without it, the computer’s parts cannot communicate or function together. It is also called the mainboard or system board, it is the main circuit board of a computer. It connects, controls, and allows communication among all major hardware components such as the CPU, RAM, storage devices, and peripherals.[5]

A motherboard is easily identified, as it is the largest board inside a computer’s casing. In tower computer’s, it stands vertically on the tower’s left or right side. While motherboards come in a variety of types and sizes– each designed to be placed in a different kind of computer and to work with different types of processors and memory– they all have the same basic components. The base of any motherboard is made of a hard sheet of nonconductive material, usually plastic.[6]

When you turn your computer on, the power supply transfers electricity to the motherboard to be used by the computer. Data is transported between the chipset components via data buses and travels between the southbridge and northbridge sections.

The data connections to the CPU, RAM, or PCIe are made through the northbridge component. The operations performed by the RAM are first “interpreted” by the CPU as being output after the RAM begins to deliver inputs to the CPU. After being written to the PCIe, the data is either copied or moved to the expansion card, based on the kind of card you have.

The data connection to the basic input/output system (BIOS), the universal serial bus (USB), the serial advanced technology attachment (SATA), and the PCI bus are managed by the southbridge component. Your computer can start up because of signals sent to the BIOS, and the data sent to the SATA “awakens” your optical, hard disc, and solid-state drives. The video card, network card, and sound card receive power from the information stored on the SATA.

The remaining components interact via an electrical signal, which serves as a hub for them. These data buses pass via a microchip’s northbridge or southbridge elements, which then branch off to other components like the CPU, RAM, PCI, and PCIe, amongst other elements.

The information sent over buses will be encoded using a programming language (1 and 0). When a signal is sent to a motherboard from one of its components, the motherboard will process it and translate it into a language the other component can comprehend. On most of today’s computing systems, all of this will occur in a split second, and there is almost no delay between the input and the output.[5]

Connectivity - refers to the motherboard’s ability to link all major computer components so they can communicate and work together. It connects the CPU (the computer’s main processor), RAM (temporary working memory), storage devices like SSDs/HDDs (where files are permanently stored), the GPU (graphics processor), and peripherals (devices like keyboard and mouse). The motherboard acts as the central hub where all these parts exchange information.

sections.content:
Every time you open an app, browse the web, or run a program, the Random Access Memory (RAM) is working behind the scenes to keep everything fast and smooth.

Think of RAM as your computer’s short-term brain—the more brain power it has, the better it can think, remember, and multitask!

Random Access Memory (RAM) is one of the most important components in Computer Hardware Servicing (CHS) because it directly affects a computer’s speed and overall performance. RAM serves as the computer’s temporary working memory, where data and programs that are currently in use are stored, so the processor can access them quickly. When a computer is powered on, the operating system and active applications are loaded into RAM, allowing tasks such as opening files, browsing the internet, and running software to be performed smoothly. However, once the computer is shut down, all data stored in RAM is erased, which is why it is referred to as volatile memory.[7]

In practical terms, RAM can be compared to a study desk: the larger the desk, the more books and materials can be opened at the same time without clutter. Similarly, a computer with higher RAM capacity can run multiple applications simultaneously with less lag or freezing. RAM works much faster than storage devices such as hard disk drives (HDDs) or solid-state drives (SSDs), which are used for long-term data storage. While storage keeps files permanently, RAM focuses on speed and efficiency, ensuring that the CPU can quickly retrieve the information it needs to perform tasks.[7]

Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM) - is typically used for a computer’s main memory. It needs continuous power to retain stored data. DRAM is cheaper than Static Random Access Memory (SRAM), has a higher density, but it produces more heat, consumes more power and is not as fast as SRAM. Each DRAM cell stores a positive or negative charge held in an electrical capacitor. This data must be constantly refreshed with an electronic charge every few milliseconds to compensate for leaks from the capacitor. A transistor serves as a gate, determining whether a capacitor’s value can be read or written.

Double Data Rate (DDR) - The first generation, released around 2000, offering twice the bandwidth of SDRAM by transferring data on both clock edges, operating at ~2.5V.

DDR2 (2nd Generation) - Faster bus speeds than DDR1, lower voltage (1.8V), and a 4n prefetch buffer, released around 2003.

DDR3 (3rd Generation) - Focused on power efficiency with lower voltage (1.5V/1.35V), higher clock speeds (800-2133 MHz), and a larger 8n prefetch buffer, released around 2007.

sections.content:
Where does a computer save files? The answer is storage devices. These components store the operating system, applications, and user data, making them essential to computer operations. You will learn different types of storage devices, how they work, and their role in Computer Hardware Servicing.[11] You will also gain basic skills in identifying, installing, and maintaining storage devices–important tasks.

Computer storage devices, classified as primary (temporary) or secondary (persistent), are essential hardware components that retain data, instructions, and information. The primary, most common devices are Hard Disk Drives (HDDs) for large capacity and Solid-State Drives (SSDs) for high-speed, durable storage. Other forms include USB flash drives, memory cards, optical discs (CD/DVD), and cloud storage.[12][13]

While it may at first seem like the range of storage options available to you is vast, they largely fall under three main categories:

Internal physical storage on your computer—your computer’s built-in storage space, often referred to as its hard drive.

External physical storage devices—similar to the memory built into your computer, only you can remove it and connect it to another device.

Cloud storage—platforms like Google Drive allow you to store files over the internet, rather than on physical storage, meaning any compatible device with an internet connection can access the files.[13]

To help you to understand which type of storage is right for your needs, let’s dive into each in a little more detail and walk through some examples.

This ultimately refers to your device’s built-in memory. Most computers and handheld devices will come with some degree of internal storage. It’s also common to be able to choose how much internal storage you want when buying a laptop, for example.

sections.content:
A Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) is a specialized electronic circuit designed to rapidly process and render images, videos, and 3D animations by executing parallel mathematical calculations. Often referred to as a graphics card, video card, or visual processing unit, it offloads tasks from the CPU to accelerate gaming, video editing, and AI workloads. It is important because it enhances display performance, supports high-resolution monitors, and accelerates demanding applications such as simulations, multimedia editing, and even modern tasks like artificial intelligence and data processing.[16][17]

A GPU has its own random access memory (RAM), an electronic memory used to store code and data that the chip can access and alter as needed. Advanced GPUs typically have RAM that has been built to hold the large data volumes required for compute-intensive tasks such as graphics editing, gaming or AI/ML use cases.

Discrete GPUs - Discrete GPUs, or dGPUs, are graphics processors that are separate from a device's CPU, where information is taken in and processed, allowing a computer to function. Discrete GPUs are typically used in advanced applications with special requirements, such as editing, content creation or high-end gaming. They are distinct chips with connectors to separate circuit boards and attached to the CPU by using an express slot.

One of the most widely used discrete GPUs is the Intel Arc brand, built for the PC gaming industry.

Integrated GPUs - An integrated GPU, or iGPU, is built into a computer or device's infrastructure and typically slotted in next to the CPU. Designed in the 2010s by Intel, integrated GPUs became more popular as manufacturers such as MSI, ASUS and Nvidia noticed the power of combining GPUs with CPUs rather than requiring users to add GPUs by a PCI express slot themselves. They remain a popular choice for laptop users, gamers and others who are running compute-intensive programs on their PCs.

Virtual GPUs - Virtual GPUs, or vGPUs, have the same capabilities as discrete or integrated GPUs but without the hardware. They are software-based versions of GPUs built for cloud instances and can be used to run the same workloads. Also, because they have no hardware, they are simpler and cheaper to maintain than their physical counterparts.[17]

sections.content:
In the world of building and upgrading PCs, one component that often doesn't get the spotlight it deserves is the PSU. In fact, when people think of powerful PCs, they often focus on the GPU or CPU. However, without a reliable PSU, even the best PC components can fail. The PSU is like the heart of your computer, pumping power to every component. A bad PSU can lead to system instability, crashes, and even hardware damage.

A computer power supply unit (PSU) is an internal hardware component that converts high-voltage alternating current (AC) from a wall outlet into low-voltage direct current (DC) necessary for components like the motherboard, CPU, and graphics card. It acts as the heart of the computer, providing stable, regulated power to ensure safe and reliable operation.[18][19]

AC to DC Conversion: at first step PSU intake the alternating current (AC) from the standard electrical outlet which is different for different. The standard electrical voltage in the Philippines is 220 volts (V) with a frequency of 60 hertz (Hz), then further, this alternating current (AC) is passed through a transformer to step it up or down according to the requirement, transformer make the current ready for the upcoming step.

Rectification: The process of rectification is based on the concept that current flows only in positive direction in a diode, in the process power supply uses the rectifiers (diodes) to convert the alternating current into vibrating direct current. The whole process is called rectification.

Smoothing: The current prepared by rectification is a vibrating direct current , which is not suitable for directly powering the computer components. Capacitors are used for making the vibrating direct current smooth which make the direct current even more suitable for the powering of the computer components.

Voltage Regulation: Despite passing through the process of smoothing, there may still have some fluctuations. Voltage regulators are installed to ensure a steady and precise output voltage. The function of this circuit is to adjust the voltage in order to meet the specifications required by the electronic components. The voltage is basically around 3v -12v depending on specific requirements of electronic components.

Output: At last, the regulated direct current (DC) voltage is then supplied according to the requirement to all components of the electronic device like motherboard, hard drives, graphics cards and so on, the output voltage or power is provided to different parts of the device using different connectors and cables.[19]

Linear Power Supply - it regulates the output voltage by dissipating the excess electrical energy as heat using simple electronic components. Because it does not use complex switching, its design is straightforward, making it reliable and easy to maintain. However, this simplicity comes at the cost of efficiency–linear PSUs tend to waste more energy and generate more heat compared to other designs. They are typically found in low-powered or specialized systems where stable, low-noise power is more important than efficiency.[18]

sections.content:
A cooling system in a computer is a set of hardware components designed to control and reduce the heat generated by internal parts such as the CPU, GPU, power supply, and motherboard during operation. As these components work, they produce heat, and without proper cooling, excessive temperatures can cause performance throttling, system instability, or permanent hardware damage. The cooling system helps maintain safe operating temperatures so the computer can run efficiently and reliably.[20]

Fans - The most popular type of cooling for computers is spinning fans that blow hot air away from the PC’s components and draw in cool air. Fans are extremely popular for consumer-grade PCs, but they have several disadvantages. They can easily spread dust and microbes in their operating environment. This makes them unsuitable for any workspace that requires cleanliness, such as hospitals or clean rooms. Additionally, the constant spinning of their fans can cause reliability issues; a broken fan can easily lead to the overheating issues we’ve previously discussed. Lastly, the continuous noise of their spinning can be distracting or uncomfortable for people nearby, such as patients trying to sleep. For these reasons, cooling fans are not an ideal solution in the medical or industrial sector.[21]

Liquid cooling - It works on a similar principle to air cooling, but with liquid as the medium. A liquid cooling system mounts directly onto the heat source, such as the CPU, and uses a heatsink to transfer heat from the processor to the liquid. The liquid is then pumped away from the heatsink, cooled, and pumped back to the heatsink in a continuous cycle. While extremely effective, liquid cooling has several notable downsides. First, it is the most expensive of all cooling options. Second, it requires very specialized equipment that takes up lots of space inside the PC, which is a serious issue for roles that require a smaller form factor. Lastly, liquid cooling systems require periodic maintenance for their numerous moving parts and can be very delicate, which makes them a poor fit for any application requiring ruggedness. For these reasons, liquid cooling is typically only seen in dedicated computing roles, such as for dedicated servers or high-end gaming PCs.[20][21]

Fanless cooling - Also known as radiative cooling, fanless cooling relies on heatsinks and radiation fans to disperse heat from the processors and into the air. A heatsink is attached to the heat source, draws heat from it, and radiates it into the surrounding air. These heatsinks are designed with as much surface area as possible and can radiate large amounts of heat despite being comparatively small. The greatest advantage of fanless cooling over other options is that it requires no moving parts and doesn’t consume electricity to do its job. For these reasons, fanless cooling is extremely popular for industrial-grade computers that need to be as rugged and simple as possible to work on or near heavy machinery. Their lack of moving parts also makes computers with fanless cooling nearly silent during operations, meaning they’re more comfortable to be around for long periods of time. Fanless cooling systems also don’t need holes in the computer’s casing to vent air through, meaning that it’s easier to make the case watertight. These factors are invaluable for medical computers, which operate for hours on end next to patients and must be frequently cleaned and disinfected.[21]

Airflow within a computer case is essential for maintaining an optimal operating environment for the system’s components. Properly managed airflow not only helps in keeping components cool but also enhances the longevity and performance of the system.

Temperature Control: Modern computer components, especially CPUs and GPUs, generate significant amounts of heat. Without effective airflow, this heat can accumulate, causing components to throttle or even fail.

System Longevity: Excessive temperatures can shorten the lifespan of computer parts. Maintaining a cooler internal environment can prolong the operational life of these components.

Performance: Many computer components will throttle, or reduce their performance, when they get too hot. Efficient airflow ensures that components stay cool and can operate at their maximum capability.[32]

sections.content:
Input devices are hardware peripherals that send data, commands, and control signals to a computer, allowing users to interact with and operate it. They convert physical input (keystrokes, mouse clicks, voice) into machine-readable digital signals.[23]

The main purpose of an input device is to serve as a bridge between the user and the computer, which it achieves through several key functions:

Data Input: Input devices transform real-world input, like keystrokes or scanned images, into digital data that a computer can process.

Command Execution: They let users control the computer, such as opening applications, selecting options, or navigating menus.

User Interface Interaction: Input devices enable engagement with graphical interfaces, allowing users to point, click, type, or gesture to interact with software effectively.[22][23]

Keyboard - The most widely used input device, designed for entering text, numbers, and commands. It includes alphanumeric keys, function keys, control keys, and special keys.[image-46]

Mouse - A pointing device that allows users to move the cursor, select items, click buttons, and navigate graphical user interfaces (GUI) efficiently.[image-47]

Joystick - Used mainly for gaming and simulations, allowing directional control of objects or characters on the screen.[image-48]

sections.content:
An output device is any computer hardware component that receives processed data from a computer and translates it into a form that is perceivable by humans, such as visual (screens), auditory (speakers), or physical (printed). These devices represent the final step in the information processing cycle, converting digital information into user-readable output.

Output devices are essential components of a computer system that deliver processed data to the user in a human-readable form—be it visual, auditory, or physical. If input devices are how a computer receives information (like typing or clicking), output devices are how it responds or communicates back.[24]

An output device is hardware that takes processed digital data from a computer and converts it into a form humans can perceive or use.

Data Processing – The computer’s CPU processes information and prepares it for output.

Signal Transmission – The processed data is sent as digital signals to the output device through an interface.

Conversion – The output device converts these digital signals into a human-perceivable form.

Presentation – The information is presented so the user can interpret or use it.[25]

Input (data entry) → Processing (CPU) → Output (results via output devices)

sections.content:
A peripheral device is defined as a computer device, such as a keyboard or printer, that is not part of the essential computer (i.e., the memory and microprocessor). These auxiliary devices are intended to be connected to the computer and used. Any various devices (including sensors) used to enter information and instructions into a computer for storage or processing and to deliver the processed data to a human operator or, in some cases, a machine controlled by the computer. Such devices make up the peripheral equipment of modern digital computer systems.[27][28]

Peripheral devices are important for enhancing functionality of a computer. Here's why peripheral devices are so important:

Enhanced Functionality: Peripheral devices expand a computer's capabilities, enabling it to perform various tasks.

User Interaction: Input devices like keyboards and mice allow users to control and interact with the system.

Data Output: Output devices such as monitors and printers display or present processed data.

Data Storage: Storage devices ensure important data is saved and can be accessed or backed up when needed.

Communication: Communication devices enable network connectivity, allowing the computer to communicate with other systems or networks.

Increased Efficiency: Peripherals improve the overall efficiency and usability of the computer by adding specialized functions.[29]

ModuleTitle: Ang Computer at ang mga Bahagi Nito
Description:
Ang araling ito ay nagpapakilala sa mga mag-aaral sa mga pangunahing bahagi na bumubuo sa isang computer system. Ipinaliliwanag nito ang layunin at tungkulin ng bawat bahagi—parehong hardware at software—at kung paano sila nagtutulungan upang iproseso ang impormasyon.


Mga Layunin:

Layunin ng araling ito na:

1. Tukuyin at ilarawan ang hardware at software ng isang computer.

2. Magpatupad ng simpleng troubleshooting para sa mga karaniwang problemang nararanasan.

3. Ipakita ang tamang pagpapanatili at pinakamahuhusay na gawi o pamamaraan.

sections.title: Paksa 1: Ang mga Bahagi ng Hardware ng Computer

sections.content:
Ang pag-aaral ng mga batayan ng computer ay maaaring mukhang komplikado sa loob, ngunit bawat device—mula sa pinakamaliit na smartphone hanggang sa pinakamalaking desktop—ay nakasalalay sa parehong mahahalagang building blocks. Ang mga building blocks na ito ay tinatawag na mga hardware components, ang pisikal na bahagi ng computer na nakikita at nahahawakan.[1][2]

Lahat ay kasama sa computer hardware mula sa central processing unit (CPU) hanggang sa random access memory (RAM), mga storage device (gaya ng hard disk drives at solid-state drives), motherboards, power supplies, at mga input/output devices (gaya ng keyboards, mice, at printers). Ang bawat bahagi ay may tiyak na tungkulin at mahalagang papel sa kabuuang pagganap ng computer.[1][2]

A. Ang Computer Bilang Hardware

Ang computer ay isang elektronikong aparato na tumatanggap ng datos, nagsasagawa ng mga operasyon, nagpapakita ng resulta, at nag-iimbak ng datos o resulta kung kinakailangan. Ito ay kombinasyon ng mga hardware at software resources na nagsasama-sama upang magbigay ng iba’t ibang kakayahan sa gumagamit. Ang hardware ay ang pisikal na bahagi ng computer, gaya ng processor, memory devices, monitor, keyboard, at iba pa, samantalang ang software ay isang hanay ng mga programa o instruksyon na kailangan ng mga hardware resources upang gumana nang maayos.[1]

B. Ang Computer at ang mga Bahagi Nito:

Sa araling ito, matututuhan mo ang iba’t ibang bahagi at komponent na bumubuo sa isang desktop computer unit. Mahalaga para sa atin na malaman ang mga batayang bahagi ng computer hardware upang mapagtibay ang iyong pag-unawa at makasabay sa mga susunod na aralin sa ModuLearn. Kasama nito, matututuhan mo rin ang kanilang mga tungkulin at bahagi. Upang higit kang maihanda sa iyong pagkatuto, narito ang isang overview ng mga tatalakayin natin sa aralin:

  • Central processing unit (CPU) - kilala bilang “utak” ng computer dahil isakatuparan nito ang mga instruksyon at isinasagawa ang mga utos.
  • Motherboard - naglalaan ng kaayusan para sa lahat ng ibang komponent at nagdurugtong sa kanila, habang nagsisilbi ring daan upang maipamahagi ang kuryente, maihatid ang impormasyon, at makakonekta sa mga device gaya ng printer o mouse.
  • Random Access Memory (RAM) - dito pansamantalang nakalagay ang data habang ginagamit ng mga programa, gaya ng kapag nagbukas ka ng application sa computer.
  • Storage Devices - mahalagang hardware na nagse-save at kumukuha ng digital data tulad ng mga dokumento, larawan, at programa, para sa pansamantala o pangmatagalang gamit.
  • Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) - espesyal na processor na mabilis na nagmamaniobra at nagbabago ng memory para mapabilis ang paggawa ng mga larawan, video, at animation na nakikita sa screen.
  • Power Supply - nagbibigay ng kuryente sa lahat ng bahagi ng computer.
  • Cooling System - sistema ng computer na tumutulong para hindi mag-overheat o uminit nang sobra.
  • Input Devices - anumang hardware na nagpapadala ng data, utos, o signal papunta sa computer (hal. keyboard, mouse).
  • Output Devices - anumang hardware na kumukuha ng naprosesong data mula sa computer at ipinapakita ang resulta sa gumagamit (hal. monitor, printer).
  • Hard drive - storage device na ginagamit para mag-imbak ng files, programa, o iba pang impormasyon sa computer.
  • Monitor - electronic device na nagpapakita ng mga programang tumatakbo sa computer.
  • Printer - makina na gumagawa ng kopya ng text o larawan sa papel gamit ang tinta.[2]

sections.content:
A. Ano ang CPU?

Alam mo ba na ang computer ay hindi gagana kung walang CPU, kahit na may monitor, keyboard, at mouse ito? Katulad ng utak ng tao, ang CPU ang kumokontrol sa lahat ng gawain ng computer. Kung wala ito, hindi makakapag-isip, makakapag-compute, o makakasagot ang system.

Sa computer hardware servicing, mahalagang malaman kung paano gumagana ang CPU dahil kailangan ito sa pag-install, troubleshooting, pag-upgrade, at maintenance. Ito ang pangunahing bahagi ng computer na nagpo-proseso ng lahat ng utos mula sa mga programa at kumokontrol sa lahat ng operasyon ng hardware.[3]

B. Pangunahing Bahagi ng CPU

  1. Arithmetic/Logic Unit
  • Ito ang bahagi ng CPU na gumagawa ng mga kalkulasyon at nagdedesisyon base sa mga paghahambing. Ang kakayahan nito sa math ay nakabatay sa apat na operasyon: addition (pagdaragdag), subtraction (pagbabawas), multiplication (pagmumultiply), at division (paghahati). Ang mga logical operations naman ay karaniwang may kinalaman sa paghahambing (kung mas malaki, mas maliit, o pantay ang isang bagay sa iba) na konektado sa isang aksyon ng computer.[3][4] Sa madaling salita, ang ALU ang “calculator at decision maker” ng CPU.
  1. Control Unit (CU)
  • Ang Control Unit ay parang manager o traffic controller sa loob ng CPU. Nasa control unit ang mga circuitry (mga electronic pathways kung saan dumadaloy ang kuryente para makontrol ang mga bahagi) na gumagabay sa computer system gamit ang mga electrical pulses (maliliit na signal ng kuryente na nagdadala ng utos). Ito rin ang nagbibigay-abiso para maisagawa ang mga high-level na utos mula sa mga programa o software.[3][4] Sa madaling salita, ang control unit ang nagsasabi sa lahat ng bahagi ng computer kung ano ang gagawin at kailan ito gagawin.
  1. Memory Unit
  • Ang Memory Unit ang namamahala sa data at mga utos habang gumagana ang CPU. Isa sa mga tungkulin nito ay ang pamamahala ng daloy ng impormasyon sa pagitan ng RAM at CPU, pati na rin ang paggamit ng cache memory (maliit pero mabilis na memory na nag-iimbak ng madalas gamitin na data). Nasa Memory Unit ang lahat ng klase ng data at panuto na kailangan para sa data processing, at nagbibigay din ito ng proteksyon para hindi magulo o mag-overlap ang data ng iba’t ibang programa.[3][4] Sa madaling salita, ang Memory Unit ang nagtatabi ng mahalagang data para mabilis at ligtas itong magamit ng CPU.

C. Mga Bahagi ng CPU

  • Cache: Ang bilis ng memory ay mahalaga sa takbo ng CPU, hindi diretsong gumagamit ng RAM ang CPU. Sa halip, ang mga modernong CPU ay may isa o higit pang layers ng cache na madalas gumagawa ng ganitong gawain dahil nakalagay ito mismo sa processor chip ng CPU. Sa mas madaling salita, ang cache ay parang mabilis na storage area para sa madalas gamitin na impormasyon.
  • Registers: Para sa agarang at tuloy-tuloy na pangangailangan ng data na dapat makuha agad para maayos ang takbo ng CPU, gumagamit ito ng registers. Ang registers ay isang uri ng permanenteng memory. Dahil nakabuo ito mismo sa loob ng CPU, puwedeng ma-access ang data sa loob ng isang kisapmata kapag kailangan. Sa madaling salita, ang registers ay nagtatago ng maliliit na piraso ng data na agad na kailangan ng CPU.
  • Clock: Mahalaga na ang kumplikadong circuitry (mga electronic pathways kung saan dumadaan ang signal) sa loob ng CPU ay gumagana nang sabay-sabay at naka-synchronize. Ang CPU clock ang nagma-manage ng process sa paraan ng pagbibigay ng electrical pulses regularly, para mag-coordinate sa iba’t ibang computer components. Ang bilis ng pagbibigay ng pulses na ito ay tinatawag na clock speed, na sinusukat sa Hertz (Hz) o Megahertz (MHz). Sa mas madaling salita, ang clock ang nagpapanatili ng tamang ritmo ng CPU para gumana nang sabay-sabay ang lahat ng bahagi sa tamang oras.
  • Instruction register and pointer: Kapag ang CPU ay nag-e-execute ng instruction set (pagkaka-sunod-sunod ng commands na kailangan niyang gawin), ang instruction pointer ay nagpapakita kung nasaan ang next instruction. Pag natapos na ang isang command, ang next information ay pumapasok sa instruction register (small storage area na nagho-hold ng current instruction), at ang susunod na command ay naka-highlight sa instruction pointer. Sa madaling salita, ito ang component na tumutulong sa CPU malaman kung ano ang ginagawa niya ngayon at ano ang susunod.
  • Buses: Ang computer bus ay may very specific role sa karamihan ng computers: ito ang nag-e-ensure ng proper data transfer at data flow (movement ng digital information) sa pagitan ng iba’t ibang computing components sa loob ng isang computer system. Ang width ng bus ay nagde-describe kung ilang bits (pinakamaliit na unit ng digital data, usually 0 or 1) ang kaya nitong i-transfer nang sabay-sabay. Ang buses ay nagbibigay ng paraan para ma-link ang CPU sa on-board memory (memory na nasa motherboard mismo) at may iba pa itong gamit.[3][4] Sa mas simpleng paliwanag, ang buses ay parang mga kalsada na dinadaanan ng data para makapag-travel sa pagitan ng computer parts.

D. Mga Gamit ng CPU

Ang functionality ng CPU ay hinahandle ng control unit, na tinutulungan ng computer clock para sa synchronization. Ang trabaho ng CPU ay nangyayari ayon sa isang cycle na may paulit-ulit na basic computing instructions.

  1. Processing Data - gumagawa ng calculations at logical operations.
  2. Executing Instructions - nagra-run ng commands mula sa software at operating system.
  3. Controlling System Operations - nakikipag-communicate sa memory, storage, at input/output devices.[4]

E. Paano nagana ang CPU (Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle)

  1. Fetch - kinukuha ang instructions mula sa memory.
  2. Decode - iniintindi kung ano ang ibig sabihin ng instruction o tina-translate ang binary instructions into electrical signals na nag-a-activate ng ibang parts ng CPU.
  3. Execute - nangyayari kapag ang computer ay nag-i-interpret at nagca-carry out ng set of instructions mula sa program.[4]

sections.content:
A. Ano ang Motherboard?

Kung ang CPU ay ang utak, ang motherboard naman ay parang nervous system ng katawan. Kung wala ito, hindi makakapag-communicate at magwo-work together ang parts ng computer. Tinatawag din itong mainboard o system board, at ito ang main circuit board ng computer. Dito nagco-connect, nagco-control, at nag-a-allow ng communication sa lahat ng major hardware components tulad ng CPU, RAM, storage devices, at peripherals.[5]

Madaling ma-identify ang motherboard dahil ito ang pinakamalaking board sa loob ng computer casing. Sa tower computers, naka-vertical ito sa left o right side ng tower. Iba-iba ang types at sizes ng motherboards—depende kung anong klase ng computer at kung anong processors at memory ang compatible—pero lahat sila ay may basic components. Ang base ng motherboard ay gawa sa matigas na nonconductive material, na kadalaasan ay plastic.[6]

B. Paano Gumagana ang Motherboard?

Kapag binuksan mo ang computer, ang power supply ay nagta-transfer ng electricity papunta sa motherboard para magamit ng computer. Ang data ay dumadaan sa chipset components sa pamamagitan ng data buses at nagta-travel sa southbridge at northbridge sections.

Ang data connections papunta sa CPU, RAM, o PCIe ay dumadaan sa northbridge. Ang operations ng RAM ay unang ini-interpret ng CPU bilang output pagkatapos magsimula ang RAM na magbigay ng inputs. Kapag naisulat na sa PCIe, ang data ay either kinokopya o nililipat sa expansion card, depende sa klase ng card.

Ang data connections papunta sa BIOS (basic input/output system), USB (universal serial bus), SATA (serial advanced technology attachment), at PCI bus ay hinahandle ng southbridge. Mag-start up ang computer dahil sa signals papunta sa BIOS, at ang data papunta sa SATA ay “nagigising” sa optical drive, hard disk, at solid-state drives. Ang video card, network card, at sound card ay kumukuha ng power mula sa information na naka-store sa SATA.

Ang natitirang components ay nag-i-interact gamit ang electrical signals, na nagsisilbing hub para sa kanila. Ang data buses ay dumadaan sa microchip’s northbridge o southbridge, tapos nagba-branch papunta sa ibang components tulad ng CPU, RAM, PCI, at PCIe.

Ang information na dumadaan sa buses ay naka-encode sa programming language (1 at 0). Kapag may signal na galing sa isang component papunta sa motherboard, ipoproseso ito ng motherboard at ita-translate sa language na maiintindihan ng ibang component. Sa karamihan ng modern computers, nangyayari lahat ito in a split second, halos walang delay sa pagitan ng input at output.[5]

C. Key Functions & Components:

  1. Connectivity - tumutukoy sa ability ng motherboard na i-link lahat ng major computer components para makapag-communicate at mag-work together. Kinokonekta nito ang CPU (main processor ng computer), RAM (temporary working memory), storage devices tulad ng SSDs/HDDs (kung saan naka-store permanently ang files), GPU (graphics processor), at peripherals (devices tulad ng keyboard at mouse). Ang motherboard ang nagsisilbing central hub kung saan nag-e-exchange ng information ang lahat ng parts.
  2. Data Highway - nagsisilbing data highway ang motherboard na nag-a-allow sa information na mag-travel sa pagitan ng computer components. Nangyayari ito sa pamamagitan ng printed circuits (maninipis na copper pathways sa motherboard na nagdadala ng electrical signals). Ang circuits na ito ang nagmo-move ng digital data sa CPU, memory, storage, at iba pang hardware.
  3. CPU Socket - may CPU socket ang motherboard, ito ang slot kung saan ini-install ang processor. Pisikal nitong hinahawakan ang CPU at nagbibigay ng electrical connections para makapag-communicate ito sa ibang parts ng computer.
  4. Expansion Slots (PCIe) - nagbibigay ng expansion slots ang motherboard para makapag-install ng additional hardware components na mag-e-expand ng capabilities ng computer. Karamihan ng modern systems ay gumagamit ng PCIe (Peripheral Component Interconnect Express) na sumusuporta sa high-speed expansion cards. Mga halimbawa nito ay graphics cards, Wi-Fi cards, at sound cards.
  5. Storage Connectors (SATA, M.2) - ginagamit ang storage connectors para i-attach ang storage devices tulad ng hard drives at solid-state drives sa motherboard. Ang SATA (Serial Advanced Technology Attachment) ay kadalasang gumagamit ng cables para i-connect ang drives, habang ang M.2 ay nag-a-allow ng drives na direktang i-plug sa motherboard. Ang connectors na ito ang nag-a-allow sa computer na mag-store ng operating system, programs, at user files.
  6. Input/Output Ports - ang Input/Output (I/O) ports ay mga connectors sa back panel ng motherboard na nag-a-allow sa external devices na makakonekta sa computer. Kasama dito ang USB ports para sa devices, audio ports para sa speakers at microphones, at Ethernet ports para sa network connections. Sila ang nag-e-enable ng communication sa pagitan ng computer at external hardware.
  7. Power Distribution - tumutukoy sa paraan kung paano nagdi-distribute ng electricity ang motherboard sa iba’t ibang computer components. Ang power mula sa PSU (power supply unit) ay dinadala sa pamamagitan ng circuits sa motherboard papunta sa CPU, RAM, at storage devices. Tinitiyak nito na bawat component ay nakakatanggap ng tamang electrical power para gumana.
  8. Firmware (BIOS/UEFI) - ang firmware ay low-level software na naka-store sa motherboard na nagco-control ng basic hardware operations. Ang BIOS (Basic Input/Output System) at UEFI (Unified Extensible Firmware Interface) ay nag-i-initialize ng hardware kapag nag-start ang computer. Sila rin ang nagra-run ng system checks at naglo-load ng operating system sa startup.

sections.content:
Tuwing nag-o-open ka ng app, nagba-browse sa web, o nagra-run ng program, ang Random Access Memory (RAM) ay tahimik na nagtatrabaho para maging mabilis at smooth ang lahat.

Isipin mo ang RAM bilang short-term brain ng computer—mas malaki ang brain power, mas magaling itong mag-isip, mag-alala, at mag-multitask!

A. Ano ang RAM?

Ang Random Access Memory (RAM) ay isa sa pinaka-importanteng components sa Computer Hardware Servicing (CHS) dahil directly nitong naaapektuhan ang speed at overall performance ng computer. Ang RAM ang nagsisilbing temporary working memory ng computer, kung saan naka-store ang data at programs na currently ginagamit, para mabilis ma-access ng processor. Kapag naka-on ang computer, ang operating system at active applications ay niloload sa RAM, kaya nagiging smooth ang tasks tulad ng pag-open ng files, pag-browse ng internet, at pag-run ng software. Pero kapag naka-shutdown ang computer, lahat ng data sa RAM ay nabubura—kaya tinatawag itong volatile memory.[7]

Sa madaling salita, puwede mong i-compare ang RAM sa study desk: mas malaki ang desk, mas maraming books at materials ang puwedeng naka-open nang sabay nang hindi magulo. Ganoon din sa computer—mas mataas ang RAM capacity, mas kaya nitong mag-run ng multiple applications nang sabay-sabay na walang lag o freeze. Ang RAM ay mas mabilis kaysa storage devices tulad ng HDDs (hard disk drives) o SSDs (solid-state drives) na ginagamit para sa long-term data storage. Habang ang storage ay nagpe-preserve ng files permanently, ang RAM ay naka-focus sa speed at efficiency, para mas mabilis makuha ng CPU ang information na kailangan nito para mag-perform ng tasks.[7]

B. Types of RAM

  1. Dynamic Random Access Memory (DRAM) - karaniwang ginagamit bilang main memory ng computer. Kailangan nito ng continuous power para ma-retain ang stored data. Mas mura ang DRAM kaysa sa Static Random Access Memory (SRAM), mas mataas ang density, pero mas mainit, mas malakas kumonsumo ng power, at hindi kasing bilis ng SRAM.

Bawat DRAM cell ay nag-i-store ng positive o negative charge na naka-hold sa isang electrical capacitor. Ang data na ito ay kailangang i-refresh regularly gamit ang electronic charge kada ilang milliseconds para ma-compensate ang leaks mula sa capacitor. Ang transistor ang nagsisilbing gate na nagde-determine kung ang value ng capacitor ay puwedeng i-read o i-write.

1. Double Data Rate (DDR) - Ito ang first generation, na-release around 2000. Nag-offer ito ng twice ng bandwidth ng SDRAM dahil ito ay nagta-transfer ng data sa parehong clock edges, at nag-o-operate sa ~2.5V.
  2. DDR2 (2nd Generation) - Mas mabilis ang bus speeds kaysa DDR1, mas mababa ang voltage (1.8V), at may 4n prefetch buffer. Na-release ito around 2003.
  3. DDR3 (3rd Generation) - Nakatuon sa power efficiency dahil mas mababa ang voltage (1.5V/1.35V), mas mataas ang clock speeds (800–2133 MHz), at mas malaking 8n prefetch buffer. Na-release ito around 2007.
  4. DDR4 (4th Generation) - Mas mababa pa ang voltage (1.2V), mas mataas ang density, at mas mabilis ang speed (2133–3200+ MHz). Na-release ito around 2014, at sinundan ng DDR5 noong 2021.[10]
  5. Static Random Access Memory (SRAM) - Itong type ng RAM ay karaniwang ginagamit para sa system’s high-speed cache. Katulad ng DRAM, kailangan din ng SRAM ng constant power para ma-hold ang data, pero hindi nito kailangan ng continuous refresh gaya ng DRAM. Mas mahal ang SRAM kaysa DRAM at mas mababa ang density, pero mas kaunti ang heat, mas mababa ang power consumption, at mas maganda ang performance.[8]
  6. Synchronous DRAM (SDRAM) - Itong type ng computer memory ay naka-synchronize sa system clock ng computer para mas mabilis at efficient ang data transfer sa CPU. Malaking improvement ito kumpara sa older asynchronous DRAM. Gumagamit ito ng pipelining at bank interleaving para makapagbigay ng mas mataas na data rates. Naging standard ito para sa PCs at servers at eventually nag-evolve sa modern types ng DDR SDRAM.[9]

Ang pag-intindi sa iba’t ibang types ng RAM ay nakakatulong para makapili ng compatible memory modules, makapag-upgrade, at makapag-troubleshoot ng memory-related problems nang mas epektibo sa real-world servicing tasks.

sections.content:
A. Para saan ang mga Storage Devices?

Saan nagsa-save ng files ang computer? Ang sagot ay sa storage devices. Ang mga components na ito ang nag-i-store ng operating system, applications, at user data—kaya essential sila sa computer operations. Matututuhan mo ang iba’t ibang types ng storage devices, paano sila gumagana, at ano ang role nila sa Computer Hardware Servicing (CHS).[11] Makakakuha ka rin ng basic skills sa pag-identify, pag-install, at pag-maintain ng storage devices—mga importanteng tasks.

Ang computer storage devices ay naka-klasipika bilang primary (temporary) o secondary (persistent). Sila ay essential hardware components na nagre-retain ng data, instructions, at information. Ang pinaka-common na primary devices ay Hard Disk Drives (HDDs) para sa large capacity at Solid-State Drives (SSDs) para sa high-speed, durable storage. May iba pang forms tulad ng USB flash drives, memory cards, optical discs (CD/DVD), at cloud storage.[12][13]

B. Main Types of Storage Devices

Sa unang tingin, parang sobrang dami ng storage options, pero nahahati sila sa tatlong main categories:

  • Internal physical storage - ito ang built-in storage space ng computer, kadalasang tinatawag na hard drive.
  • External physical storage devices—katulad ito ng memory na nakapaloob sa computer, pero ito ay removable at puwedeng i-connect sa ibang device.
  • Cloud storage—Ito ay mga platform tulad ng Google Drive na nag-a-allow mag-store ng files over the internet. Hindi ito physical storage, kaya puwedeng ma-access ang files mula sa kahit anong compatible device na may internet connection.[13]

Para mas maintindihan kung alin ang tamang storage para sa pangangailangan mo, iisa-isahin natin ang bawat type at magbibigay ng examples.

C. Internal Physical Storage

Ito ay tumutukoy sa built-in memory ng device mo. Karamihan ng mga computer at handheld devices ay may internal storage. Madalas din na puwede kang pumili kung gaano kalaki ang internal storage na gusto mo kapag bumibili ng laptop.

May dalawang main types ng internal storage—Hard Disk Drives (HDDs) at Solid-State Drives (SSDs)—pero may ibang devices na may kombinasyon ng dalawa. Hindi tulad ng cloud storage na puwede mong idagdag sa ibang oras, ang pagpili ng internal storage ay isang importanteng factor kapag pumipili ng device.[13]

  1. Hard Disk Drive (HDD) - Ang HDD ay ang built-in storage ng computer. Kung matagal mo nang ginagamit ang device, malaki ang posibilidad na naka-store na ang mga files mo sa HDD nito. Bilang traditional type ng storage, gumagamit ang hard disk ng spinning disks para magbasa at magsulat ng data. Ang HDD ay bagay para sa malalaking storage needs tulad ng TV recorders at servers. Kahit hindi ito kasing bilis o kasing reliable ng modern solutions, advantage nito ang pagiging mas mura at mas malaki ang storage capacity.
  2. Solid-state drive (SSD) - Ang solid-state technology ay evolution ng traditional HDD. Sa halip na spinning disks na madaling masira, gumagamit ang SSD ng flash memory para sa mas mabilis at mas reliable na performance nang walang moving parts. Ibig sabihin, mas mabilis ang SSD at mas matibay kaysa HDD. Karaniwan itong makikita sa modern PCs, smartphones, at tablets. Pero dahil mas mabilis at reliable, mas mahal ito kaysa HDD at kadalasan mas maliit ang storage capacity.[13]

• Parehong storage devices ang SSD at HDD, pero magkaiba sila kung paano nag-i-store at nag-a-access ng data: Ang SSD ay gumagamit ng non-volatile flash memory na walang moving parts, kaya mas mabilis ang read/write speeds, mas energy efficient, at mas durable. Samantalang ang HDD ay umaasa sa spinning magnetic platters at mechanical read/write heads, kaya mas mabagal, mas madaling masira physically, pero mas mura per gigabyte at available sa mas malalaking capacities. Dahil dito, ang SSD ay ideal para sa workloads na nangangailangan ng high performance at madalas na data access, habang ang HDD ay mas pinipili para sa large-volume storage kung saan hindi critical ang speed at mas importante ang cost.[14]

D. External Physical Storage

Puwede kang magkaroon ng parehong HDD at SSD bilang external hard drives. Karaniwan, sila ang may pinakamalaking storage capacity sa mga external options. Pareho ang purpose ng external HDDs at SSDs sa internal counterparts—nag-i-store ng data—pero magkaiba ang paraan ng trabaho nila sa loob. Dahil dito, mas mabilis at mas reliable ang SSD kaysa HDD. Popular ang SSDs kapag kailangan ng mabilis na file access at data transfer. Karamihan ng external hard drives ay puwedeng i-connect sa kahit anong computer; hindi sila naka-tie sa isang device, kaya magandang solution para sa pag-transfer ng files across devices.[13]

  • Flash Memory Devices - Kasama dito ang USB flash drives at memory cards. Reliable sila para sa pag-transport ng mas maliliit na files. Compatible sila sa karamihan ng devices na may USB ports, pero ang newer computers minsan ay nangangailangan ng adapter. Kahit na may mas advanced na options ngayon, popular pa rin sila dahil madali gamitin at portable.[13]
  • Legacy Storage - Hindi na ito madalas gamitin araw-araw, pero may mga lumang external storage methods na puwede pa ring magamit depende sa files at devices.

sections.title: Mga Halimbawa ng legacy external storage formats

sections.content:
• Optical media – tulad ng CDs, DVDs, at Blu-ray discs. Karamihan ng devices ngayon ay wala nang disk drive, kaya kailangan ng external device para magamit sila.
  • Floppy discs – halos obsolete na, pero dati ay common method ng file storage bago naging default ang CDs bilang external storage ng mga PCs noong late 1990s.[13]

Generally, hindi mo na gagamitin ang legacy external storage media. Pero kung nagtatrabaho ka sa older technology, baka kailanganin mo pa ring gumamit ng ganitong formats.

E. Cloud storage

Ang cloud storage ay isang service na nag-a-allow sa users na mag-store ng files at data sa remote servers (powerful computers na nag-store at nagma-manage ng data) na maa-access gamit ang internet, imbes na i-save lang sa local device. Dahil dito, puwede kang mag-access, mag-upload, mag-download, at mag-share ng files mula sa iba’t ibang devices basta may internet connection. Nang lumabas ang cloud storage, nagbago ang paraan ng paggamit natin ng files at data. Ang “cloud” ay hindi isang lugar o object, kundi malaking koleksyon ng servers na nasa data centers sa iba’t ibang parte ng mundo. Kapag nag-save ka ng document sa cloud, naka-store ito sa mga servers na iyon.

Dahil online naka-store ang lahat, hindi nito ginagamit ang built-in storage ng computer mo, kaya nakaka-save ka ng space.

Mas malaki rin ang storage capacity ng cloud storage kumpara sa USB flash drives at iba pang physical options. Hindi mo na kailangang maghanap sa iba’t ibang devices para sa tamang file dahil lahat ay accessible in one place. At kung kailangan mo ng mas malaking space, hindi mo na kailangang bumili ng bagong drive—puwede ka lang mag-upgrade ng cloud storage plan.[13]

• Space efficiency—nakaka-free up ng computer storage.
  • High capacity—mas mataas ang storage capacity kaysa USB flash drives at iba pang physical options.
  • Portability—accessible mula sa kahit anong device na may internet connection, perfect para sa on-the-go users. Puwede ring gawing available offline ang files.
  • Security and backup—naka-backup ang data mo at accessible kahit saan at kahit kailan, basta may internet access.[15]

questions.question: What is the primary role of a GPU?
Why is PSU called the “heart”?

sections.content:
A. Ano ang GPU?

Ang Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) ay isang specialized electronic circuit na designed para mabilis mag-process at mag-render ng images, videos, at 3D animations gamit ang parallel mathematical calculations. Madalas itong tawaging graphics card, video card, o visual processing unit. Ang GPU ay nag-o-offload ng tasks mula sa CPU para mapabilis ang gaming, video editing, at AI workloads. Importante ito dahil pinapaganda nito ang display performance, sumusuporta sa high-resolution monitors, at pinapabilis ang demanding applications tulad ng simulations, multimedia editing, at modern tasks gaya ng artificial intelligence at data processing.[16][17]

B. Paano Gumagana ang GPU?

May sariling random access memory (RAM) ang GPU, isang electronic memory na ginagamit para mag-store ng code at data na puwedeng ma-access at ma-modify ng chip kapag kailangan. Ang advanced GPUs ay may RAM na specially built para mag-hold ng large data volumes na kailangan sa compute-intensive tasks tulad ng graphics editing, gaming, o AI/ML use cases.

C. Iba’t-Ibang Uri ng GPU

  • Discrete GPUs - Discrete GPUs, o dGPUs, ay graphics processors na hiwalay sa CPU ng device. Kinukuha nila ang information at pinoproseso, kaya nakaka-function ang computer. Karaniwang ginagamit ang discrete GPUs sa advanced applications na may special requirements tulad ng editing, content creation, o high-end gaming. Sila ay distinct chips na may connectors papunta sa separate circuit boards at naka-attach sa CPU gamit ang express slot. Isa sa mga widely used discrete GPUs ay ang Intel Arc brand na ginawa para sa PC gaming industry.
  • Integrated GPUs - Ang integrated GPU ay built-in sa computer o device infrastructure at karaniwang naka-slot sa tabi ng CPU. Na-design noong 2010s ng Intel, naging popular ito nang mapansin ng manufacturers tulad ng MSI, ASUS, at Nvidia ang advantage ng pag-combine ng GPU sa CPU imbes na kailangan pang magdagdag ng GPU sa pamamagitan ng PCI express slot. Popular pa rin ito sa laptop users, gamers, at iba pang gumagamit ng compute-intensive programs sa PCs.
  • Virtual GPUs - Ang virtual GPUs ay may parehong capabilities ng discrete o integrated GPUs pero walang hardware. Software-based versions sila ng GPUs na ginawa para sa cloud instances at puwedeng gamitin para sa parehong workloads. Dahil wala silang physical hardware, mas simple at mas mura silang i-maintain kaysa sa physical counterparts.[17]

questions.question: Which type of power supply is MOST commonly used?
Which components generate heat?

sections.content:
A. Para saan ang power supply?

Sa mundo ng PC building at upgrading, madalas hindi nabibigyan ng spotlight ang PSU. Kadalasan, kapag iniisip ng tao ang powerful PC, nakatuon sila sa GPU o CPU. Pero kung walang reliable PSU, puwedeng mag-fail kahit ang best components. Ang PSU ay parang puso ng computer, nagpu-pump ng power sa bawat component. Kapag bad PSU, puwedeng mag-lead sa system instability, crashes, at hardware damage.

Ang computer power supply unit (PSU) ay internal hardware component na nagko-convert ng high-voltage alternating current (AC) mula sa wall outlet papunta sa low-voltage direct current (DC) na kailangan ng motherboard, CPU, at graphics card. Parang puso ng computer, nagbibigay ito ng stable at regulated power para safe at reliable ang operation.[18][19]

B. Paano Gumagana ang Power Supply Unit (PSU)?

  • AC to DC Conversion: Unang step, kinukuha ng PSU ang alternating current (AC) mula sa electrical outlet. Sa Pilipinas, standard voltage ay 220 volts (V) at frequency ay 60 hertz (Hz). Pagkatapos, dumadaan ito sa transformer para ma-step up o step down ayon sa requirement. Ginagawa ng transformer na ready ang current para sa susunod na proseso.
  • Rectification: Ang rectification ay proseso kung saan ang AC ay kino-convert papunta sa direct current (DC). Ginagamit dito ang rectifiers (diodes) na nagpapadaloy ng kuryente sa positive direction lang. Ang resulta ay vibrating DC.
  • Smoothing: Ang vibrating DC ay hindi pa suitable para diretsong mag-power ng computer components. Kaya ginagamit ang capacitors para gawing smooth ang current, para mas maging angkop sa pag-supply ng power sa components.
  • Voltage Regulation: Kahit na-smoothed na ang current, puwede pa ring may fluctuations. Kaya may voltage regulators para siguraduhin na steady at precise ang output voltage. Ina-adjust ng circuit ang voltage para tumugma sa requirements ng electronic components. Karaniwang nasa 3V–12V ang voltage depende sa specific needs ng components.
  • Output: Sa huli, ang regulated DC voltage ay dini-deliver sa lahat ng components ng computer tulad ng motherboard, hard drives, at graphics cards. Ang power ay ipinapasa gamit ang iba’t ibang connectors at cables ayon sa pangangailangan ng bawat bahagi.[19]

C. Types of Power Supply Units

  1. Linear Power Supply - Nagre-regulate ito ng output voltage sa pamamagitan ng pag-dissipate ng excess electrical energy bilang heat gamit ang simple electronic components. Dahil hindi ito gumagamit ng complex switching, straightforward ang design kaya reliable at madaling i-maintain. Pero ang simplicity na ito ay may kapalit: hindi efficient ang linear PSUs, mas maraming energy ang nasasayang at mas maraming heat ang napo-produce kumpara sa ibang designs. Karaniwang makikita ito sa low-powered o specialized systems kung saan mas importante ang stable at low-noise power kaysa efficiency.[18]
  2. Switching Power Supply - Ito ang madalas gamitin sa modern computers dahil gumagamit ito ng high-frequency electronic switches para mas efficient ang voltage regulation. Mas kaunti ang heat na napo-produce at mas magaan kumpara sa linear PSUs. Widely used ito sa desktop computers, laptops, at karamihan ng electronic devices dahil kaya nitong mag-deliver ng power nang mas effective at maka-adapt sa iba’t ibang power requirements nang hindi nagiging bulky.[18]

D. PSU Design: Modular vs. Non-Modular

  1. Non-Modular Power Supply - Lahat ng power cables ay permanently attached sa unit. Dahil dito, mas simple at kadalasang mas mura. Pero ang downside ay hindi puwedeng tanggalin ang mga unused cables, kaya nagiging cluttered ang loob ng computer case. Nakakaapekto ito sa airflow at nagpapahirap sa cable management.[18]
  2. Modular Power Supply - Pinapayagan nito ang users na ikabit lang ang cables na kailangan para sa specific system build nila. Dahil dito, mas maayos ang cable management, mas maganda ang airflow, at mas neat ang loob ng system. Useful ito lalo na para sa custom builds at upgrades. Pero, modular designs ay kadalasang mas mahal kaysa non-modular.[18]

Ang PSU ang power house ng computer system, responsible sa pagbibigay ng kuryente para gumana nang maayos ang lahat ng components. Habang umuunlad ang technology, patuloy ding nag-i-evolve at nag-u-upgrade ang PSUs.[18]

sections.content:
A. Para Saan ang Cooling System?

Ang cooling system sa computer ay set ng hardware components na designed para i-control at bawasan ang init na nagge-generate ng internal parts tulad ng CPU, GPU, power supply, at motherboard habang ginagamit. Habang nagtatrabaho ang mga components na ito, nagpo-produce sila ng heat. Kung walang proper cooling, puwedeng magdulot ng sobrang init na nagreresulta sa performance throttling (bumabagal ang takbo), system instability, o permanenteng hardware damage. Ang cooling system ang tumutulong mag-maintain ng safe operating temperatures para makatakbo ang computer nang efficient at reliable.[20]

B. Types of Cooling System

  1. Fans - Ang pinaka-popular na cooling system para sa computers ay spinning fans na nagbo-blow ng hot air palabas ng PC components at nagda-draw ng cool air papasok. Sobrang popular ito sa consumer-grade PCs, pero may ilang disadvantages. Madali nitong ma-ikalat ang dust at microbes sa paligid, kaya hindi ito suitable sa workspaces na kailangan ng cleanliness tulad ng hospitals o clean rooms. Bukod pa dito, ang constant spinning ng fans ay puwedeng magdulot ng reliability issues—kapag nasira ang fan, puwedeng mag-overheat ang computer. At ang continuous noise ng fans ay puwedeng maging distracting o uncomfortable para sa mga tao sa paligid, gaya ng patients na sinusubukang matulog o natutulog. Dahil dito, cooling fans ay hindi ideal solution sa medical o industrial sector.[21]
  2. Liquid cooling - Gumagana ito sa similar principle ng air cooling, pero liquid ang medium. Ang liquid cooling system ay naka-mount direkta sa heat source tulad ng CPU, at gumagamit ng heatsink para i-transfer ang init mula sa processor papunta sa liquid. Ang liquid ay pinapump palayo sa heatsink, pinapalamig, at ibinabalik sa heatsink sa continuous cycle. Sobrang effective ito, pero eto ay may mga downsides: una, ito ang pinaka-expensive option. Pangalawa, kailangan nito ng specialized equipment na kumakain ng maraming space sa loob ng PC, kaya hindi bagay sa roles na kailangan ng maliit na form factor. Pangatlo, kailangan ng periodic maintenance dahil sa moving parts at delicate design, kaya hindi ito bagay sa rugged applications. Dahil dito, ang liquid cooling ay karaniwang ginagamit lang sa dedicated servers o high-end gaming PCs.[20][21]
  3. Fanless cooling (Radiative Cooling) – Nakilala rin bilang radiative cooling, umaasa ito sa heatsinks at radiation fins para i-dispersed ang init mula sa processors papunta sa hangin. Ang heatsink ay naka-attach sa heat source, kumukuha ng init mula dito, at nagra-radiate papunta sa surrounding air. Ang heatsinks ay ginawa na may malaking surface area para makapag-radiate ng maraming halaga ng init kahit maliit ang size. Ang pinaka-advantage ng fanless cooling ay wala itong moving parts at hindi kumukonsumo ng electricity para gumana. Dahil dito, sobrang popular ito sa industrial-grade computers na kailangan rugged at simple para gumana malapit sa heavy machinery. Dahil walang moving parts, halos silent ang computers na may fanless cooling habang nag-o-operate, kaya mas comfortable gamitin nang matagal. Hindi rin kailangan ng butas sa computer casing para mag-vent ng hangin, kaya mas madaling gawing watertight ang case. Sobrang valuable ito para sa medical computers na nag-o-operate nang matagal sa tabi ng patients at kailangang madalas linisin at i-disinfect.[21]

C. Importance of Airflow

Ang airflow sa loob ng computer case ay essential para mapanatili ang optimal operating environment ng system components. Kapag maayos ang airflow, hindi lang nito pinapalamig ang components, nakakatulong din ito sa pagpapahaba ng lifespan at pagpapaganda ng performance ng system.

  1. Temperature Control: Ang modern computer components, lalo na ang CPUs at GPUs, ay nagge-generate ng sobrang init. Kung walang effective airflow, naiipon ang init na puwedeng magdulot ng throttling (pagbagal ng takbo) o kahit failure ng components.
  2. System Longevity: Ang sobrang init ay puwedeng magpaiksi ng lifespan ng computer parts. Kapag mas malamig ang internal environment, mas tumatagal ang operational life ng components.
  3. Performance: Maraming computer components ang nagti-throttle o bumababa ang performance kapag sobrang init. Ang efficient airflow ay nakakatulong para manatiling cool ang components at makapag-operate sila sa maximum capability.[32]

questions.question: What is a peripheral device?
Which of the following is NOT considered a peripheral device?

sections.content:
A. Ano ang input device?

.Ang input devices ay hardware peripherals na nagpapadala ng data, commands, at control signals sa computer, para makapag-interact at makapag-operate ang user. Kino-convert nila ang physical input (tulad ng keystrokes, mouse clicks, o voice) papunta sa machine-readable digital signals.[23]

Ang role ng input device ay magsilbing tulay sa pagitan ng user at computer, na ginagawa nila sa pamamagitan ng ilang key functions:

  • Data Input: Binabago ng input devices ang real-world input (tulad ng keystrokes o scanned images) papunta sa digital data na puwedeng i-process ng computer.
  • Command Execution: Pinapahintulutan nila ang user na makontrol ang computer, gaya ng pag-open ng applications, pagpili ng options, o pag-navigate sa menus.
  • User Interface Interaction: Nagbibigay sila ng paraan para makipag-engage sa graphical interfaces. Puwede kang mag-point, mag-click, mag-type, o gumamit ng gestures para makipag-interact nang effective sa software.[22][23]

B. Common Types of Input Devices

  1. Keyboard - Pinaka-ginagamit na input device, dinisenyo para mag-enter ng text, numbers, at commands. May alphanumeric keys, function keys, control keys, at special keys.
  2. Mouse - Isang pointing device na nagbibigay-daan sa users na igalaw ang cursor, mag-select ng items, mag-click ng buttons, at mabisang makapag-navigate ng graphical user interfaces (GUI).
  3. Joystick - Karaniwang ginagamit sa gaming at simulations, nagbibigay ng directional control sa objects o characters sa screen.
  4. Light Pen - Isang pen-shaped input device na puwedeng gamitin para mag-select o mag-drawing direkta sa monitor screen. Madalas gamitin sa design at CAD applications.
  5. Trackball - Isang stationary pointing device kung saan iniikot ng user ang bola para igalaw ang cursor. Useful ito sa environments na limited ang desk space.
  6. Scanner - Nagko-convert ng printed documents, images, at photos papunta sa digital format, para maging editable at storable sa computer.
  7. Graphics Tablet (Digitizer) - Nagbibigay-daan sa artists at designers na mag-drawing o magsulat digitally gamit ang stylus. Karaniwang ginagamit sa graphic design at animation.
  8. Microphone - Nag-ca-capture ng audio input, kino-convert ang tunog papunta sa digital data para sa voice commands, communication, at audio recording.[22]

sections.content:
A. What is an output device?

Ang output device ay anumang computer hardware component na tumatanggap ng processed data mula sa computer at kino-convert ito sa form na nakikita, naririnig, o nahahawakan ng tao—tulad ng visual (screens), auditory (speakers), o physical (printed). Ito ang final step sa information processing cycle, kung saan ang digital information ay nagiging user-readable output.

Ang output devices ay mahalagang components ng computer system dahil sila ang nagde-deliver ng processed data sa user sa human-readable form—visual, auditory, o physical. Kung ang input devices ang paraan ng computer para makatanggap ng information (tulad ng pag-type o pag-click), ang output devices naman ang paraan ng computer para mag-respond o makipag-communicate pabalik.[24]

B. Paano Gumagana ang Output Device?

Ang output device ay hardware na kumukuha ng processed digital data mula sa computer at kino-convert ito sa form na puwedeng makita, marinig, o magamit ng tao.

  • Data Processing – Ang CPU ng computer ang nagpo-process ng information at inihahanda ito para sa output.
  • Signal Transmission – Ang processed data ay ipinapadala bilang digital signals papunta sa output device sa pamamagitan ng interface.
  • Conversion – Ang output device ang nagko-convert ng digital signals papunta sa pormat na naiintindihan ng tao.
  • Presentation – Ipinapakita o ipinapresenta ang information para magamit o ma-interpret ng user.[25]

Input (data entry) → Processing (CPU) → Output (results via output devices)

C. Common Types of Output Devices

  1. Monitor - Isang device na nagdi-display ng visual information tulad ng text at graphics na pinoproseso ng computer.
  2. Printer - Isang device na gumagawa ng physical copies ng processed digital information (halimbawa: text o images) sa papel.
  3. Speaker - Isang device na nagko-convert ng digital audio signals mula sa computer papunta sa tunog na naririnig ng tao.
  4. Projector - Isang device na kumukuha ng visual information mula sa computer at ipinapakita ito sa mas malaking surface para makita ng mas marami.
  5. Headphones - Personal audio output devices na nagde-deliver ng tunog mula sa computer direkta sa tenga ng user.[26]

questions.question: Which port is primarily used to connect a computer to a local area network (LAN) or the internet?
Which port transmits data one bit at a time and is mostly considered a legacy port today?

sections.content:
A. Ano ang isang peripheral device?


Isang aparatong peripheral ay binibigyang kahulugan bilang isang aparato sa computer, tulad ng keyboard o printer, na hindi bahagi ng computer (ibig sabihin, ang memorya at microprocessor). Ang mga pantulong na aparatong ito ay nilayong ikonekta sa computer at gamitin. Anumang iba't ibang mga aparato (kabilang ang mga sensor) na ginagamit upang magpasok ng impormasyon at mga tagubilin sa isang computer para sa pag-iimbak o pagproseso at upang maihatid ang naprosesong data sa isang operator ng tao o, sa ilang mga kaso, isang makinang kontrolado ng computer. Ang mga naturang aparato ay bumubuo sa peripheral na kagamitan ng mga modernong digital na sistema ng computer.[27][28]

B. Kahalagahan ng mga Peripheral Device

Mahalaga ang mga peripheral device para sa pagpapahusay ng paggana ng isang computer. Narito kung bakit napakahalaga ng mga peripheral device:

  • Pinahusay na Pag-andar (Enhanced Functionality): Pinalalawak ng mga peripheral device ang kakayahan ng isang computer, na nagbibigay-daan dito upang maisagawa ang iba't ibang gawain.
  • Interaksyon ng Gumagamit (User Ineteraction): Ang mga input device tulad ng mga keyboard at mouse ay nagbibigay-daan sa mga user na kontrolin at makipag-ugnayan sa system.
  • Paglabas ng Datos (Data Output): Ang mga output device tulad ng mga monitor at printer ay nagpapakita o nagpepresenta ng mga naprosesong datos.
  • Pag-iimbak ng Datos (Data Storage): Tinitiyak ng mga storage device na naka-save ang mahahalagang data at maaaring ma-access o ma-backup kung kinakailangan.
  • Komunikasyon (Communication): Ang mga aparatong pangkomunikasyon ay nagbibigay-daan sa pagkakakonekta sa network, na nagpapahintulot sa computer na makipag-ugnayan sa ibang mga sistema o network.
  • Nadagdagang Kahusayan (Increased Efficiency): Pinapabuti ng mga peripheral ang pangkalahatang kahusayan at kakayahang magamit ang computer sa pamamagitan ng pagdaragdag ng mga espesyal na function.[29]

C. Mga Opsyon sa Pagkonekta ng mga Peripheral Device

Maaaring kumonekta ang mga peripheral device sa isang computer sa pamamagitan ng iba't ibang paraan. Ang bawat isa ay nag-aalok ng iba't ibang bilis, saklaw, at compatibility. Kabilang sa mga karaniwang opsyon sa pagkonekta ang:

  • USB (Universal Serial Bus): Karaniwan ang USB para sa mga keyboard, mouse, printer, at mga external storage device; sinusuportahan nito ang parehong wired at wireless na koneksyon.
  • Bluetooth: Ang Bluetooth ay wireless na koneksyon para sa mga device tulad ng headphone, speaker, at mouse.
  • Wi-Fi: Ginagamit ang Wi-Fi para sa mga wireless networked device tulad ng mga printer at camera.
  • HDMI (High-Definition Multimedia Interface): Ang HDMI ay nagkokonekta ng mga monitor, projector, at TV para sa high-definition na video at audio.
  • Ethernet: Koneksyong naka-wire para sa komunikasyon sa network, kadalasan para sa internet o LAN access.
  • Thunderbolt: Mabilis na koneksyon para sa mga panlabas na device tulad ng mga storage drive at monitor.
  • VGA (Video Graphics Array): Ang VGA ay isang mas lumang koneksyon para sa mga monitor o projector, na sumusuporta sa mas mababang resolution ng video.
  • Display Port: Katulad ng HDMI, ginagamit para sa pagkonekta ng mga high-definition monitor.[29]

D. Ano ang port o konektor ng computer?

Mga port ng computer ay mga connection points sa isang desktop na nagbibigay-daan sa iyong makipag-ugnayan sa mga panlabas na device, tulad ng mga peripheral device. Ang mga pisikal na konektor na ito ay nagsisilbing mga gateway para sa paglilipat ng data, paghahatid ng kuryente, at komunikasyon sa pagitan ng iyong computer at mga peripheral tulad ng mga monitor, keyboard, printer, at mga panlabas na storage device.[30]

E. Kahalagahan ng mga Port ng Kompyuter

  • Koneksyon (Connectivity): Pinapayagan ka ng mga ito na ikonekta ang iba't ibang uri ng mga panlabas na device sa iyong computer.
  • Pagpapalawak (Expandability): Binibigyang-daan ka ng mga port na magdagdag ng functionality sa iyong computer system kung kinakailangan.
  • Paglilipat ng datos (Data Transfer): Maraming port ang nagpapadali sa mabilis na paglilipat ng data sa pagitan ng mga device.
  • Mga kakayahan sa multimedia (Multimedia Capabilities): Ang mga video at audio port ay nagbibigay-daan sa iyong kumonekta sa mga external display at sound system.
  • Networking: Ang mga Ethernet port ay nagbibigay ng mabilis at maaasahang koneksyon sa internet.[30]

F. Mga Uri ng Port ng Kompyuter

  • Internal Port - Ang mga panloob na port ay ginagamit upang ikonekta ang mga panloob na aparato tulad ng mga disk drive, CD drive, at iba pang mga panloob na aparato sa motherboard.
  • External Port - Ang mga panlabas na port ay ginagamit upang ikonekta ang mga panlabas na aparato: modem, mouse, printer, flash drive, atbp. sa motherboard.[31]
  1. Serial Port (COM Port) - Isang communication port para sa pagkonekta ng mga lumang external device. Nagpapadala ito ng data nang paisa-isa at may mga modelong 9-pin o 25-pin.
  2. Parallel Port - Naglilipat ng maraming bits ng data nang sabay-sabay. Madalas na ginagamit para sa pagkabit ng mga lumang device gamit ang 25-pin connector.
  3. USB (Universal Serial Bus) Port - Isang maraming gamit na port na sumusuporta sa maraming panlabas na device at nagbibigay-daan sa mga plug-and-play na koneksyon na may high-speed transfer.
  4. PS/2 Port - Isang 6-pin port na orihinal na ginagamit para sa pagkonekta ng mga keyboard at mouse sa mga lumang computer.
  5. VGA Port - Isang video port na may 15 butas na ginagamit upang ikonekta ang isang display device sa video card ng computer.
  6. Mga Socket - Mga interface point na ginagamit upang ikonekta ang mga audio device tulad ng mga mikropono o speaker sa sound card ng computer.
  7. FireWire Port (IEEE 1394) - Isang high-speed port para sa paglilipat ng malalaking halaga ng data, kadalasang ginagamit para sa mga kagamitan sa video.
  8. Infrared Port - Isang wireless communication port na nagpapadala at tumatanggap ng mga infrared signal sa loob ng maikling sakop nang walang mga kable.
  9. Game Port - Ginamit noon upang ikonekta ang mga gaming controller; ngayon ay higit na napapalitan ng USB.
  10. Modem Port - Nagkokonekta ng modem ng computer sa network ng telepono para sa komunikasyong dial-up.
  11. DVI (Digital Video Interface) Port - Isang video port na karaniwang ginagamit upang ikonekta ang mga flat-panel monitor sa graphics card ng computer.
  12. Ethernet Port - Isang network port na nagkokonekta sa computer sa LAN o Internet sa pamamagitan ng cable sa bilis na mula 10 hanggang 1000 Mbps.[31]
